rulings.Case Title: MARK ROBINSON III VS. SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1038
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT, DON GRIFFIN, LAURIE SCOLARI's Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1038. Denied. The motion is untimely per Code of Civil Procedure 1038(c). In addition, defendants fail to demonstrate that plaintiff commenced and maintained this action in bad faith or that the action lacked reasonable cause. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TOM SANDERS VS. PG&E CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT PG&E CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PG&E's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1and 3, denied as to issue 4, and taken off calendar as moot as to issue 2. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden as to the wrongful termination claim. Defendant relies on the declaration of Paul Roller to argue that plaintiff was terminated because of the power tools incident and subsequent cover up. However, Mr. Roller does not state the precise reason for the termination. The undisputed facts show that the defamation claim is time-barred per Code of Civil Procedure 340(c) and barred by the common interest privilege of Civil Code 47(c). Plaintiff's request for a continuance of the hearing is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC. VS. RINCON RESIDENTIAL TOWERS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Disco Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 2, PETITIONER ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC.'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CASSAUNDRA ELLENA VS. STANDARD INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Req For Production Of Docs; Separate Statement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 11, PLAINTIFF CASSAUNDRA ELLENA's motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents. The court adopts the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Huibonhoa as follows: Granted as to Requests 28-30. The scope shall be limited to documents provided to Sonoma and related to the underlying policy. Given the broad scope of discovery, these requests reasonably could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Standard does not offer any clear objections to production of these documents other than relevance and overbreadth. To the extent there is confidential, proprietary, or private information in the requested documents, the protective order the parties enter into can protect those interests. The court adds a production date of October 26, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP A NEW VS. JOAN MACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions; Memo P/A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 21, PLAINTIFF KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP's Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Pro Tem Judge Watson as follows: Denied. Considering the need of both parties to be fully conversant with the facts of the case to prepare for trial or settlement negotiations (and considering that the services at issue were performed on behalf of an individual not a party to the action) the need for full disclosure outweighs the annoyance of responding to more than 35 special interrogatories. No costs or fees are awarded to any party.The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 23, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DERRICK RICHARDS WHITE VS. U.C.S.F. MEDICAL CENTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 23, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with twenty days leave to amend. At an ex parte hearing on October 5 Mr. White stated that he intended to file an amended complaint and wished more time to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YEE LIN SUKI POON et al VS. DENNIS KIT DONG UNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANTs DENNIS UNG, MELINDA HOANG's MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Granted in its entirety with ten days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will modify the proposed order submitted by the moving parties to conform to the tentative ruling and, as so modified, will sign it without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YEE LIN SUKI POON et al VS. DENNIS KIT DONG UNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 9, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANTs DENNIS UNG, MELINDA HOANG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to fourth, eight and ninth causes of action No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will modify the proposed order submitted by the moving parties to conform to the tentative ruling and, as so modified, will sign it without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)
